scenes.summary: Studio logos and an opening text crawl explain the historical context of the Roman Empire and the ongoing war in Germania.
A hand brushes through a field of wheat. Maximus stands in a snowy forest, looking contemplative before the upcoming battle.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Remembers Home Before Battle
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene begins with a dreamlike close-up of a hand gently brushing the tops of golden wheat in a sunlit field. It then transitions to a close-up of Roman General Maximus, clad in armor and a fur cloak, looking down with a somber expression. A small bird perches on a bare branch nearby. Maximus looks up, a fleeting smile crossing his face as he remembers his home, before his gaze hardens in preparation for the reality of war.

dialogue_highlights: No dialogue

trivia.text: Before Russell Crowe was cast as Maximus, several other actors were considered for the role, including Antonio Banderas, Tom Cruise and Mel Gibson, who turned down the role as he was already committed to The Patriot (2000).
trivia.match_reasoning: Russell Crowe is seen playing the role of Maximus in the clip.

trivia.text: The opening battle scene set in the forests of Germania was shot at Bourne Wood, near Farnham, Surrey, in England.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows Maximus standing in the snowy forest of Germania just before the opening battle.

trivia.text: The scenes of Maximus' farm were filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip begins with the iconic shot of a hand brushing through the wheat, which represents Maximus' farm.

scenes.summary: Maximus walks through the Roman camp, inspecting the troops and speaking with his officers. A horse returns carrying the headless body of the Roman envoy, and the Germanic tribesmen taunt the Romans, prompting Maximus to order the attack.

content_desc.clip_title: Roman Army Prepares for Battle in Germania
content_desc.summary_paragraph: General Maximus leads the Roman army as they amass in the dark, muddy forests of Germania. He walks among his troops, assessing their readiness and morale before the impending conflict. A rider returns from the enemy lines, and a Germanic warrior throws a severed Roman head, signaling their refusal to surrender. In response, Maximus prepares his men and gives the final order to attack.

setting.location: Muddy, deforested battlefield near a dense forest in Germania

dialogue_highlights: People should know when they're conquered.
Strength and honor.
At my signal, unleash hell.

trivia.text: The opening battle scene set in the forests of Germania was shot at Bourne Wood, near Farnham, Surrey, in England.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the opening battle scene, explicitly identified by the on-screen text 'GERMANIA', taking place in a forest.

trivia.text: When Scott learned that the Forestry Commission was planning to remove a section of the forest, he obtained permission to burn it down for the scene.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in the forest mentioned in the fact, and smoke and fires are visible in the background as the army prepares for battle.

scenes.summary: Maximus rides into the forest to join his cavalry, delivering a rousing speech to his men. Meanwhile, the Roman infantry and artillery prepare their weapons for the assault.

content_desc.clip_title: Roman Army Prepares for Battle in the Forest
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Roman forces prepare for a massive battle against barbarian hordes in a misty, dark forest. A commander orders the infantry and archers to form up and ready their weapons, including massive catapults. Meanwhile, General Maximus rides through the trees with his cavalry, delivering a stirring speech about duty and the afterlife. He inspires his men with the promise that their actions in life will echo in eternity, as the army readies flaming arrows for the impending clash.

dialogue_highlights: What we do in life echoes in eternity.
If you find yourself alone, riding in green fields with the sun on your face, do not be troubled. For you are in Elysium, and you're already dead!
Three weeks from now, I will be harvesting my crops.

trivia.text: The opening battle scene set in the forests of Germania was shot at Bourne Wood, near Farnham, Surrey, in England.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the opening battle scene set in a forest, where Maximus addresses his cavalry before the attack.

trivia.text: When Scott learned that the Forestry Commission was planning to remove a section of the forest, he obtained permission to burn it down for the scene.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the Roman army preparing flaming arrows and artillery to fire into the forest, which was burned down for this sequence.

scenes.summary: The Roman army unleashes a devastating barrage of flaming arrows and catapult fire on the Germanic tribes. Maximus leads a brutal cavalry charge through the forest, engaging in fierce hand-to-hand combat until the Romans emerge victorious.

content_desc.clip_title: Epic Roman Battle in the Forest
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The Roman army engages in a brutal and chaotic battle against Germanic tribes in a dark, misty forest. Flaming arrows and catapult fireballs rain down on the enemy, setting the woods ablaze. General Maximus leads a devastating cavalry charge into the enemy flank, eventually fighting fiercely on foot amidst the carnage. The Romans emerge victorious, chanting 'Roma victor' as the battle concludes.

dialogue_highlights: Archers, ignite!
Hold the line!
Roma victor.

trivia.text: The opening battle scene set in the forests of Germania was shot at Bourne Wood, near Farnham, Surrey, in England.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the opening battle scene set in the forests of Germania.

trivia.text: When Scott learned that the Forestry Commission was planning to remove a section of the forest, he obtained permission to burn it down for the scene.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features the forest being set on fire by the Roman army's flaming arrows and catapults.

trivia.text: When filming battle scenes, Scott and cinematographer John Mathieson used multiple cameras filming at various frame rates and used a 45-degree shutter, which resulted in stylized visuals similar to those found in Saving Private Ryan.
trivia.match_reasoning: The hand-to-hand combat in the clip clearly displays the stylized, stuttering visual effect created by the 45-degree shutter.

scenes.summary: Commodus and Lucilla travel in a carriage towards the Roman camp, discussing their dying father, the Emperor. Commodus arrives at the camp and immediately asks for his father's whereabouts.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus and Lucilla Arrive at the Roman Camp
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Commodus and his sister Lucilla travel in a luxurious carriage through a dense forest towards a Roman military camp. Inside, they debate whether their father, the Emperor, is truly dying, with Commodus eagerly anticipating being named his successor. Upon arriving at the muddy, bustling camp, Commodus learns the Emperor is still at the front lines and immediately calls for his horse.

dialogue_highlights: He will name me.
After two weeks on the road, your incessant scheming is hurting my head.

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix is seen playing the role of Commodus in this scene.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen is seen playing the role of Lucilla in this scene.

scenes.summary: Emperor Marcus Aurelius surveys the battlefield and commends Maximus. Commodus arrives, disappointed to have missed the fighting, and greets Maximus.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus and Marcus Aurelius After the Battle
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following a brutal and victorious battle, Emperor Marcus Aurelius commends General Maximus for his valor. When asked how he wishes to be rewarded, Maximus simply asks to go home. Soon after, the Emperor's son, Commodus, arrives late to the scene, eager to celebrate the triumph. However, Marcus Aurelius dismisses his son's grand gestures, instead directing the honor towards Maximus, creating a tense dynamic between the three men.

dialogue_highlights: Let me go home.
Save the bulls. Honor Maximus. He won the battle.
So much for the glory of Rome.

trivia.text: The opening battle scene set in the forests of Germania was shot at Bourne Wood, near Farnham, Surrey, in England.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the immediate aftermath of the opening battle scene set in the forests of Germania.

trivia.text: When Scott learned that the Forestry Commission was planning to remove a section of the forest, he obtained permission to burn it down for the scene.
trivia.match_reasoning: The burning forest, which the production obtained permission to set on fire, is visible in the background throughout the scene.

scenes.summary: During the victory celebration, Maximus joins Commodus and the senators in a tent. Commodus asks for Maximus's political support, but Maximus expresses his desire to return home.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus and Commodus Discuss Rome's Future
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following a brutal battle, General Maximus washes the blood from his hands in the Roman encampment. He is approached by Commodus and two senators who probe his political allegiances. Commodus attempts to secure Maximus's loyalty against the Senate, but Maximus expresses his desire to simply return home to his family.

dialogue_highlights: Dirt cleans off a lot easier than blood, Quintus.
The soldier has the advantage of being able to look his enemy in the eye, Senator.
Maximus, we must save Rome from the politicians, my friend.

scenes.summary: Marcus Aurelius speaks with Lucilla, expressing regret that she was not born a man to rule Rome, and asks for her help in managing Commodus.

content_desc.clip_title: Marcus Aurelius and Lucilla Discuss Family and Politics
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Emperor Marcus Aurelius visits his daughter Lucilla in her dimly lit tent. He laments that she was not born a man, believing she would have made a strong and just Caesar. He then asks for her help in guiding her brother, Commodus, hinting at his impending decisions regarding the succession. The scene ends with a bittersweet acknowledgment of their strained father-daughter relationship.

dialogue_highlights: If only you had been born a man, what a Caesar you would have made.
Let us pretend that you are a loving daughter, and I am a good father.
This is a pleasant fiction, isn't it?

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene as Lucilla, a role that Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for.

scenes.summary: Marcus Aurelius summons Maximus and asks him to become the protector of Rome after his death, intending to return power to the Senate instead of Commodus. Maximus asks for time to consider.

content_desc.clip_title: Marcus Aurelius Asks Maximus to Protect Rome
content_desc.summary_paragraph: General Maximus walks through a freezing Roman encampment after a brutal battle. He is summoned by the aging Emperor Marcus Aurelius, who is reflecting on his legacy and the corruption of Rome. Believing his own son Commodus is unfit to rule, the Emperor asks Maximus to take power upon his death and return Rome to a republic.

setting.location: Roman military camp and Emperor's tent

dialogue_highlights: There was once a dream that was Rome. You could only whisper it.
I want you to become the protector of Rome after I die.
You are the son that I should have had.

scenes.summary: Lucilla approaches Maximus outside his tent. They discuss her father's favor, her brother Commodus, and their respective children. Maximus remains guarded and reaffirms his loyalty to Rome.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus and Lucilla Reconnect in Camp
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus and Lucilla share a tense and intimate conversation in the Roman military camp. They discuss the Emperor's failing health, the impending succession, and their past relationship. The exchange reveals lingering feelings and mutual guardedness as they navigate their current roles and past history.

dialogue_highlights: I think you have a talent for survival.
I will always serve Rome.
I could always tell when you were lying, because you were never any good at it.

trivia.text: Before Russell Crowe was cast as Maximus, several other actors were considered for the role, including Antonio Banderas, Tom Cruise and Mel Gibson, who turned down the role as he was already committed to The Patriot (2000).
trivia.match_reasoning: Russell Crowe is visible in this scene playing the role of Maximus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen is visible in this scene playing the role of Lucilla.

scenes.summary: Inside his tent, Maximus prays to his ancestors for the safety of his wife and son. His servant Cicero enters, and Maximus reflects on the burden of duty.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Prays to His Ancestors
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Inside his dimly lit tent, Maximus prays to his ancestors, asking for guidance and protection for his family. As he prays, he experiences a brief, emotional vision of his wife and son. Afterwards, he speaks with his servant Cicero about the burden of duty, expressing doubt about ever returning home.

dialogue_highlights: Whisper to them, I live only to hold them again.
Sometimes I do what I want to do. The rest of the time, I do what I have to.
We may not be able to go home, after all.

trivia.text: The scenes of Maximus' farm were filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features a brief vision of Maximus' wife and son at their farm, which was filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.

scenes.summary: Marcus Aurelius informs Commodus that he will not inherit the throne, as Rome is to become a republic again under Maximus's guidance. Devastated and enraged by his father's rejection, Commodus suffocates him.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Smothers Emperor Marcus Aurelius After Succession Denial
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Emperor Marcus Aurelius informs his son Commodus that he will not inherit the throne, choosing to pass power to Maximus instead. Devastated and feeling perpetually unloved, Commodus tearfully confronts his father about his perceived shortcomings and lack of affection. As Marcus embraces him in a moment of regretful apology, Commodus tragically smothers his father to death in a fit of grief and rage.

dialogue_highlights: You will not be emperor.
Your faults as a son is my failure as a father.
I would butcher the whole world, if you would only have loved me!

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix is visibly playing the role of Commodus in this scene.

scenes.summary: Maximus is summoned to the Emperor's tent and discovers Marcus Aurelius is dead. Commodus claims it was natural causes and demands Maximus's loyalty, but Maximus refuses and walks out.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Discovers the Emperor's Death
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus is abruptly awakened and summoned to the Emperor's tent, where Commodus informs him that Marcus Aurelius has died. Maximus mourns over the Emperor's body, ignoring Commodus's demand for his loyalty. As Maximus leaves the tent in defiance, Lucilla reluctantly pledges her allegiance to the new Caesar.

dialogue_highlights: Our great father is dead.
Your emperor asks for your loyalty, Maximus. Take my hand. I only offer it once.
Hail, Caesar.

scenes.summary: Maximus returns to his tent and orders Cicero to wake the senators. Quintus arrives with Praetorian guards, arrests Maximus, and reveals that Commodus has ordered his execution.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus is Arrested and Betrayed
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus prepares to seek counsel from the senators after discovering the Emperor's death, demanding his sword and armor. He is interrupted by his former friend Quintus, who arrives armed alongside Praetorian guards. Quintus arrests Maximus on the new Caesar's orders and commands his execution, coldly stating that Maximus's family will join him in the afterlife.

dialogue_highlights: The Emperor has been slain.
The Emperor died of natural causes.
Your family will meet you in the afterlife.

scenes.summary: Maximus is taken to a snowy forest to be executed. He manages to overpower his captors, killing them all, but sustains a severe injury to his arm in the process.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Escapes Execution in the Forest
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus is led into a desolate, snowy forest by Praetorian guards to be executed. After praying for his family and requesting a soldier's death, he seizes an opportunity to disarm his executioner. A brutal fight ensues where Maximus kills the guards, grabs a sword, and escapes on horseback.

dialogue_highlights: Blessed Father, watch over my wife and son.
At least give me a clean death. A soldier's death.
The frost, sometimes it makes the blade stick.

trivia.text: The scenes of Maximus' farm were filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip begins with a brief vision of Maximus' farm, which is mentioned as being filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.

scenes.summary: Maximus rides desperately to reach his home. He arrives too late, finding his farm burned and his wife and son murdered. Overcome with grief and exhaustion, he collapses.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Discovers His Murdered Family
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus rides relentlessly across the empire, desperate to reach his family before the emperor's assassins. Intercut with his grueling journey are visions of his wife and son being approached by Roman cavalry. Upon arriving at his estate, his exhausted horse collapses, and Maximus discovers his home burned to the ground. He finds the charred, crucified bodies of his family and breaks down in agonizing grief, eventually collapsing in the dirt.

dialogue_highlights: Whisper to them I live only to hold them again. For all else is dust and air.

trivia.text: Logan rewrote much of the first act and made the decision to kill off Maximus' family to increase the character's desire for revenge.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the tragic moment Maximus discovers the bodies of his murdered wife and son, reflecting the script decision to kill off his family.

trivia.text: The scenes of Maximus' farm were filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features extensive shots of Maximus' farm, both in his visions and when he arrives to find it destroyed.

scenes.summary: Maximus awakens in a slaver's caravan traveling across the desert. A fellow captive, Juba, tends to his wounds and urges him to survive.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Captured by Slavers in the Desert
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Severely wounded and delirious, Maximus collapses in the desert and experiences surreal visions of his family and home. He is discovered by a caravan of slavers who lock him in a wooden cage for transport across the arid landscape. Inside the cage, a fellow captive named Juba tends to Maximus's festering shoulder wound using maggots, encouraging him to stay alive.

dialogue_highlights: Don't die. They'll feed you to the lions. They are worth more than we are.
You'll meet them again. Not yet.
No. They will clean it. Wait and see.

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows Maximus enslaved and traveling in a caravan across a desert landscape.

trivia.text: The scenes of Maximus' farm were filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.
trivia.match_reasoning: A brief vision of a hand brushing through a wheat field, representing Maximus' farm, is shown early in the clip.

scenes.summary: The caravan arrives at the Roman province of Zucchabar. Proximo, a lanista, inspects the slaves and purchases Maximus and Juba for his gladiator school.

content_desc.clip_title: Proximo Buys Slaves in Zucchabar
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In the dusty Roman province of Zucchabar, Proximo confronts a local slave trader over a past deal involving defective giraffes. Seeking to make amends, the trader shows Proximo his new stock of captive men. Proximo carefully inspects the slaves, taking particular interest in a strong Numidian and a stoic Spaniard bearing a legion tattoo. After a brief negotiation, Proximo purchases the men to be used in the gladiatorial arena.

dialogue_highlights: You sold me queer giraffes. I want my money back.
Some are good for fighting, others for dying.
Mark of the legion. Deserter.

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the slaves arriving in a desert town and being purchased for a gladiatorial training school, which this fact notes was filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

scenes.summary: Proximo addresses his new recruits, telling them they will die in the arena. During a training assessment, Maximus initially refuses to fight but easily disarms a veteran gladiator when pressed.

content_desc.clip_title: Proximo Addresses the New Gladiators at the School
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Proximo arrives at his gladiator training camp in a bustling desert town and addresses his newly acquired slaves. He bluntly informs them that their lives are now his to profit from through their deaths in the arena. The men are then tested in a sparring ring, where Proximo evaluates their fighting skills and marks them with paint. Maximus, dubbed the 'Spaniard', refuses to engage in the mock combat, prompting Proximo to delay his assessment.

setting.location: Gladiator training camp in a desert town

dialogue_highlights: I paid it so that I could profit from your death.
Gladiators, I salute you.
His time will come.

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip takes place at the gladiatorial training school, which this fact explicitly states was filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

trivia.text: Oliver Reed, who plays Proximo, died of a heart attack on May 2, before all his scenes had been filmed.
trivia.match_reasoning: Oliver Reed appears prominently in this scene as Proximo, introducing himself to the new recruits.

scenes.summary: In the holding cells, Maximus uses a sharp stone to scrape the legion tattoo off his arm. Juba questions him about his gods and his refusal to fight.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Removes His SPQR Tattoo
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In the dimly lit confines of a gladiator barracks, Maximus sits alone, painfully scraping away his SPQR tattoo with a sharp stone to erase his past as a Roman soldier. Juba, a fellow enslaved gladiator, approaches and questions his reluctance to fight, noting that survival requires it. Seeing the bloody wound, Juba asks if the mark is a sign of Maximus's gods and if destroying it will anger them, to which Maximus responds only with a soft, bitter chuckle.

dialogue_highlights: Spaniard, why don't you fight? We all have to fight.
Is that the sign of your gods? Will that not anger them?

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: This scene takes place in the holding cells of the gladiatorial training school, which was filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

scenes.summary: The gladiators are prepared for their first match and marked with colors to pair them up. Proximo gives them a final speech, urging them to die with dignity so they will be remembered as men.

content_desc.clip_title: Gladiators Prepare for Battle in the Arena Dungeons
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Enslaved men, marked with red paint, are led through a gruesome corridor of hanging carcasses towards the arena. In the armory below the stands, Proximo addresses the new gladiators, offering a grim perspective on their inevitable deaths and the glory of the crowd. The men are then chained together in pairs and armed, bracing themselves as the crowd above chants for blood.

dialogue_highlights: Ultimately, we're all dead men. Sadly, we cannot choose how, but we can decide how we meet that end, in order that we are remembered as men.
Thrust this into another man's flesh, and they will applaud and love you for that.

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip takes place at the gladiatorial training school, which the fact notes was filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

trivia.text: Oliver Reed, who plays Proximo, died of a heart attack on May 2, before all his scenes had been filmed.
trivia.match_reasoning: Oliver Reed, who plays Proximo, is seen delivering a speech to the gladiators in this scene.

scenes.summary: Maximus and Juba are thrust into a chaotic gladiatorial battle in a provincial arena. Maximus fights with brutal efficiency, surviving the melee and catching the attention of Proximo.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Fights in the Provincial Arena
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Chained together in pairs, Maximus and his fellow gladiators are thrust into a brutal provincial arena. Amidst the chaos and dust, they fight for their lives against armed opponents, including a man wearing a terrifying bull mask. Utilizing his military training, Maximus quickly dispatches his foes, eventually teaming up with Juba to defeat a formidable masked gladiator. The crowd cheers wildly as Maximus stands victorious, surveying the carnage.

dialogue_highlights: [No dialogue]

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The desert-based provincial arena and gladiatorial training school where this battle takes place were filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

trivia.text: Oliver Reed, who plays Proximo, died of a heart attack on May 2, before all his scenes had been filmed.
trivia.match_reasoning: Oliver Reed is shown in this scene playing Proximo, observing the gladiators from the stands.

scenes.summary: Commodus returns to Rome in triumph, though some citizens call him a usurper. In the Senate, he dismisses concerns about the plague and sanitation, instead declaring 150 days of games to win the love of the mob.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Returns to Rome and Announces Gladiatorial Games
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Commodus makes a triumphant entry into Rome, greeted by cheering crowds and a wary Senate. He quickly shows his disdain for the senators' practical concerns, preferring to focus on winning the love of the mob. To achieve this, he announces 150 days of gladiatorial games. Senator Gracchus cynically recognizes this as a brilliant manipulation, noting that the true heart of Rome lies in the Colosseum's sand.

dialogue_highlights: The beating heart of Rome is not the marble of the Senate. It's the sand of the Colosseum.
He'll bring them death, and they will love him for it.
I will give the people a vision of Rome, and they'll love me for it.

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix is prominently featured in this scene playing the role of Commodus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen is featured in this scene playing the role of Lucilla.

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: This scene is set in Rome and features multiple establishing shots of the Colosseum.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The wide and aerial shots of the Colosseum in this scene utilize the digital extensions mentioned in the fact.

scenes.summary: Maximus continues to dominate in the arena, angrily asking the crowd if they are entertained. Proximo tells Maximus that they are going to Rome to fight in the Colosseum, advising him that winning the crowd is the key to winning his freedom.

content_desc.clip_title: Are You Not Entertained?
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The gladiator known as the Spaniard brutally defeats his opponents in a provincial arena, then angrily throws his sword into the stands, shouting at the crowd. Later, his owner Proximo summons him to discuss their upcoming journey to Rome to fight in the Colosseum. Proximo explains that to win his freedom, the Spaniard must learn to win the crowd, a challenge he accepts with grim determination.

key_actions: Throwing a sword at the spectators
dialogue_highlights: Are you not entertained? Is this not why you're here?
Win the crowd, and you'll win your freedom.
I will win the crowd. I will give them something they've never seen before.

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip takes place at the gladiatorial training school and local arena in a desert setting, which were filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

scenes.summary: Maximus and Juba look out over the desert and discuss their families and the afterlife. Maximus expresses his belief that he will see his family again, but "not yet."

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus and Juba Discuss the Afterlife
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Proximo looks out over a vast desert landscape from his quarters. On the walls of the compound, Juba and Maximus share a quiet moment reflecting on their lost families. Juba speaks of his wife and daughters, knowing he will only see them in the afterlife. Maximus reveals that his own wife and son are already waiting for him, and they agree that their reunion will happen, but 'not yet'.

dialogue_highlights: Do you believe you'll see them again when you die?
My wife and my son are already waiting for me.
You'll meet them again. But not yet.

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place at the gladiatorial training school in the desert, which was filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

scenes.summary: A montage shows the gladiators traveling in wagons through various landscapes, eventually arriving at the outskirts of Rome.

content_desc.clip_title: Horse-Drawn Carriage Approaches a Grand Ancient City
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene begins with a dramatic time-lapse of a stormy sky giving way to a brilliant sunrise. The view then transitions to a rural dirt path where excited children run alongside a horse-drawn carriage. As the camera pans out, a magnificent ancient city with towering walls and classical architecture is revealed in the distance. Soldiers on horseback follow the carriage as it makes its way toward the sprawling metropolis.

setting.location: Rural road leading to an ancient city

dialogue_highlights: I see him. I knew.
Where? There he is.

scenes.summary: The wagons carrying the gladiators arrive at the Colosseum. The men are let out of their cages and marvel at the massive structure while Proximo greets an old friend.

content_desc.clip_title: Gladiators Arrive at the Colosseum
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A caravan carrying captive gladiators arrives in the bustling city of ancient Rome, making its way to the Ludus Magnus. The men are unloaded from their caged wagons and get their first awe-inspiring glimpse of the massive Colosseum towering above them. Proximo, their owner, looks upon the grand structure with nostalgia and advises Maximus that to succeed, he must win the crowd.

setting.location: Rome, Ludus Magnus, outside the Colosseum

dialogue_highlights: Bring me fortune.
I didn't know men could build such things.
Win the crowd.

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene features the characters arriving at and marveling at the Colosseum, which was partially built as a physical replica for filming.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The massive upper tiers of the Colosseum that the gladiators look up at in this scene were created using digital extensions.

scenes.summary: In the palace, Commodus watches Lucius sleep and discusses his plans to dissolve the Senate with Lucilla. He asks her to stay with him, but she refuses and leaves.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus's Ambition and Fear
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Commodus and Lucilla stand over the sleeping Lucius before moving to another room. Lucilla prepares a tonic for Commodus as he reveals his grand, ambitious plans to dissolve the Senate and make Rome a wonder. Revealing his vulnerability and unsettling attachment, Commodus asks Lucilla to stay with him because he is afraid of the dark. When she refuses, he demands a kiss, and she obliges with a kiss on the forehead before leaving him alone.

dialogue_highlights: I will make Rome the wonder of the ages.
Still afraid of the dark, brother?
Then kiss me.

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix appears in this scene as Commodus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene as Lucilla.

scenes.summary: In the Colosseum holding area, Proximo argues with Cassius about the matches, not wanting to waste his best fighters. Cassius assigns them the Battle of Carthage.

content_desc.clip_title: Gladiators Arrive at the Colosseum
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus and his fellow gladiators are marched through the crowded, bustling streets of Rome towards the imposing Colosseum. As they take in the grand spectacle of the city, their owner, Proximo, negotiates terms with a Roman official named Cassius. Proximo argues against wasting his best fighters in a reenactment of the Battle of Carthage, demanding higher pay for the risk. Cassius ruthlessly shuts him down, threatening to cancel his contract and send him back to the provinces if he complains.

dialogue_highlights: The Emperor wants battles, and I don't want to sacrifice my best fighters.
If you want to give away the best gladiators in the whole of the empire, then I want double the rates.
Then you can crawl back down that shit hole that you came from.

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features an exterior shot of the Colosseum, which was partially built as a physical replica for the film's Rome scenes.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The upper tiers of the Colosseum visible in the exterior panning shot were digitally added to the physical set.

trivia.text: Oliver Reed, who plays Proximo, died of a heart attack on May 2, before all his scenes had been filmed.
trivia.match_reasoning: Oliver Reed is prominently featured in this scene playing the character Proximo.

scenes.summary: Young Lucius visits the gladiator cells and speaks with Maximus. They talk about horses, and Lucius reveals his name and lineage.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Meets Young Lucius
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A young boy named Lucius approaches the gladiator holding cells to speak with the famous 'Spaniard'. They discuss horses and the rumors of the gladiator's immense strength. When Maximus asks about the boy's father, Lucius reveals he is dead, and Maximus learns the boy's true identity just before he is called away.

dialogue_highlights: They said you could crush a man's skull with one hand.
My father is dead.
Lucius Verus, after my father.

scenes.summary: The gladiators are equipped with armor and weapons in the armory. They are given instructions and line up in the tunnel, preparing to enter the arena.

content_desc.clip_title: Gladiators Prepare for Battle in the Armory
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus and a group of gladiators wait tensely in the dimly lit dungeons beneath the Colosseum. As they prepare for their upcoming match, Maximus performs his familiar ritual of rubbing dirt into his hands before donning a spiked helmet. A Roman guard gives them final instructions on how to properly salute the Emperor, ending with a grim command to die with honor as they march toward the arena gates.

key_actions: Marching up the stairs toward the arena light
dialogue_highlights: When the Emperor enters, raise your weapons, salute him, and then speak together.
Face the Emperor and don't turn your back on him.
Go, and die with honor.

scenes.summary: The gladiators enter the arena for the Battle of Carthage. Maximus takes command, instructing the men to stay together as the chariots attack.

content_desc.clip_title: Gladiators Enter the Colosseum for the Battle of Carthage
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A group of gladiators, including Maximus, marches into the massive Colosseum to the deafening cheers of the Roman crowd. An announcer theatrically introduces the spectacle as a recreation of the fall of Carthage, casting the gladiators as the doomed barbarian horde. Recognizing the danger, Maximus quickly takes command, instructing the men to fight as a unit to increase their chances of survival. The tension peaks as the gates burst open and heavily armed chariots charge into the arena.

key_actions: Chariots charging out of the gates
dialogue_highlights: We who are about to die salute you!
Whatever comes out of these gates, we've got a better chance of survival if we work together.
If we stay together, we survive.

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place inside the Colosseum, which was partially constructed as a physical set in Malta for the film.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The upper tiers and remaining height of the Colosseum visible as the gladiators enter the arena were added digitally.

scenes.summary: Maximus leads the gladiators to victory against the charioteers in a reenactment of the Battle of Carthage. Commodus enters the arena to meet the victorious "Spaniard," and Maximus reveals his true identity, vowing vengeance. The crowd demands Maximus live, forcing Commodus to spare him.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Reveals His Identity to Commodus in the Colosseum
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a brutal reenactment of the Battle of Carthage, Maximus leads a group of gladiators to an unexpected victory against heavily armed chariots. Intrigued by the surviving warrior, Emperor Commodus descends into the arena to meet him. When commanded to reveal his identity, Maximus removes his helmet and declares his true name, vowing vengeance against the corrupt emperor. Swayed by the roaring crowd chanting for the gladiator's life, Commodus is forced to spare him.

key_actions: Gladiators forming a defensive shield wall
Commodus confronting Maximus in the arena
Maximus removing his helmet to reveal his face
themes_and_concepts: The power of the public crowd
dialogue_highlights: My name is Maximus Decimus Meridius... father to a murdered son, husband to a murdered wife, and I will have my vengeance, in this life or the next.
My history's a little hazy, Cassius. But shouldn't the barbarians lose the battle of Carthage?
You will remove your helmet and tell me your name.

trivia.text: Logan rewrote much of the first act and made the decision to kill off Maximus' family to increase the character's desire for revenge.
trivia.match_reasoning: Maximus explicitly states he is the "father to a murdered son, husband to a murdered wife" and vows to have his "vengeance," reflecting the script decision to kill off his family.

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in the Colosseum, a partial replica of which was built in Malta for the film.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The upper portions of the Colosseum visible in the wide shots of the arena were added digitally.

scenes.summary: Commodus, visibly shaken by Maximus's survival, discusses the situation with Lucilla. He realizes the legions lied to him and contemplates his next move.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Expresses His Paranoia and Vexation to Lucilla
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a dimly lit palace chamber, Emperor Commodus expresses his deep frustration and paranoia over learning that Maximus is still alive. He questions his sister Lucilla about her feelings towards Maximus and justifies his past actions. The conversation highlights Commodus's deep-seated insecurities about respect and love from his subjects, while Lucilla carefully navigates his volatile emotional state.

dialogue_highlights: Why is he still alive? I don't know. He shouldn't be alive. It vexes me. I'm terribly vexed.
If they lie to me, they don't respect me. If they don't respect me, how can they ever love me?
I wouldn't want to be your enemy.

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix appears in this scene as Commodus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene as Lucilla.

scenes.summary: Lucilla secretly visits Maximus in his cell. She tries to convince him to meet with a senator who opposes Commodus, but Maximus, consumed by grief and anger, refuses her help, stating the man she once knew is gone.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus and Lucilla Secretly Meet in the Dungeons
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus is escorted by guards into a dark dungeon cell and shackled to the wall. Lucilla emerges from the shadows to speak with him secretly. She pleads for his help to overthrow Commodus, sharing her own terror and grief. Maximus, consumed by the loss of his family and his status as a slave, angrily rejects her offer and tells her to leave.

dialogue_highlights: My family was burnt and crucified while they were still alive.
Today I saw a slave become more powerful than the emperor of Rome.
Forget you ever knew me, never come here again.

trivia.text: Logan rewrote much of the first act and made the decision to kill off Maximus' family to increase the character's desire for revenge.
trivia.match_reasoning: Maximus explicitly mentions that his family was 'burnt and crucified,' reflecting the script decision to kill them off to motivate his revenge.

trivia.text: Before Russell Crowe was cast as Maximus, several other actors were considered for the role, including Antonio Banderas, Tom Cruise and Mel Gibson, who turned down the role as he was already committed to The Patriot (2000).
trivia.match_reasoning: Russell Crowe is seen playing the role of Maximus in this scene.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen is seen playing the role of Lucilla in this scene.

scenes.summary: The gladiators eat together in their quarters. Hagen asks Maximus about his past military victories. Proximo warns Maximus that Commodus will try to destroy his reputation before killing him.

content_desc.clip_title: Gladiators Converse in the Dungeon
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus abruptly ends his conversation with Lucilla and is returned to the gladiator holding cells. As the men eat their rations, Hagen questions Maximus about his past military victories. Juba then offers a profound observation, warning Maximus that the Emperor must destroy his legendary reputation before he can safely execute him.

dialogue_highlights: Guard! The lady is finished with me.
You commanded legions? You had many victories?
He must kill your name before he kills you.

scenes.summary: In the Colosseum, Senators Gracchus and Gaius discuss Commodus's manipulation of the mob. Cassius announces a grand match between Maximus and the undefeated Tigris of Gaul. Maximus fights Tigris in an arena filled with chained tigers, ultimately defeating him but refusing the crowd's demand to kill him.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Fights Tigris of Gaul
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In the Colosseum, Emperor Commodus orchestrates a rigged match between Maximus and the undefeated champion, Tigris of Gaul. During the intense battle, chained tigers are released from trapdoors to attack Maximus. Despite the overwhelming odds, Maximus defeats Tigris. Defying Commodus's 'thumbs down' order to execute the fallen gladiator, Maximus spares his life, earning the crowd's adoration as 'Maximus the Merciful'.

dialogue_highlights: We mortals are but shadows and dust.
We who are about to die salute you.
Maximus the Merciful!

trivia.text: Lou Ferrigno was offered the role of Tigris of Gaul, but it ultimately went to Sven-Ole Thorsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Sven-Ole Thorsen appears in this clip as Tigris of Gaul, who is introduced and fights Maximus in the arena.

trivia.text: For the fight sequence involving tigers, both real tigers and a dummy tiger were used.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features the specific fight sequence between Maximus and Tigris that involves tigers.

trivia.text: Some of the live animals were filmed on set with the actors, and some were filmed against a bluescreen and then digitally composited into the scene.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the tigers interacting with the actors during the arena fight sequence.

scenes.summary: Commodus descends into the arena to confront Maximus face-to-face. He taunts Maximus with gruesome details about the murders of his wife and son, hoping to provoke an attack. Maximus remains stoic, turns his back on the emperor, and walks away to the cheers of the crowd.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Taunts Maximus in the Arena
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following a brutal arena battle, Emperor Commodus descends to the sands to confront Maximus, surrounded by Praetorian guards. Commodus cruelly taunts Maximus with the gruesome details of his family's murder, trying to provoke him into a fatal reaction. Showing immense restraint, Maximus refuses to strike, instead delivering a cold promise that Commodus's time is running out.

dialogue_highlights: I have only one more life to take. Then it is done.
The time for honoring yourself will soon be at an end, Highness.

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in the Colosseum, a partial replica of which was built in Malta for the film.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The wide shots of the arena show the upper tiers of the Colosseum, which were added digitally.

trivia.text: For the fight sequence involving tigers, both real tigers and a dummy tiger were used.
trivia.match_reasoning: Real tigers are visible chained to the floor of the arena at the beginning of the clip.

scenes.summary: As Maximus is escorted through the tunnels beneath the Colosseum, the crowd cheers for him. He spots his former servant, Cicero, among the onlookers and urgently tells him to find him.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Reconnects with Cicero
content_desc.summary_paragraph: As Maximus is escorted through a cheering crowd outside the arena, his former servant Cicero desperately tries to get his attention. Amidst the chaos, they manage a brief exchange where Cicero reveals the army is camped at Ostia. Maximus secretly receives a small pouch from Cicero and orders him to tell the men he is alive and to find him.

key_actions: Climbing a wall to reach someone
dialogue_highlights: Where are you camped? Ostia.
Tell the men their general lives. Find me.

scenes.summary: Back in his cell, Maximus carves a small wooden figure. Juba asks him what he says to his family in the afterlife, and Maximus shares a tender thought about his son before keeping his words to his wife private.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Discusses His Deceased Family with Juba
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a dimly lit, candlelit room, Maximus holds small wooden figurines representing his deceased wife and son. Juba asks him if his family can hear him in the afterlife and what he says to them. Maximus shares a touching message for his son before playfully refusing to share what he says to his wife, leading both men to share a warm laugh.

characters: Maximus (grieving but finding a moment of levity)
dialogue_highlights: To my boy, I tell him I will see him again soon, and to keep his heels down when he's riding his horse.
To my wife... That is not your business.

scenes.summary: Commodus and Falco discuss the threat Maximus poses. Commodus decides not to kill him directly to avoid making him a martyr, but instead plans to wait for his enemies to reveal themselves.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Plots Against Maximus
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Commodus paces in a dimly lit palace room, frustrated by Maximus's growing popularity and defiance. Senator Falco advises him to kill Maximus, but Commodus refuses to make him a martyr. Instead, Commodus devises a cunning strategy, comparing himself to a sea snake that feigns injury to draw out its enemies, and orders his guards to follow every senator.

dialogue_highlights: I will not make a martyr of him.
So, we will lie still, and let our enemies come to us and nibble.

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix is visible in this scene playing the role of Commodus.

scenes.summary: Cicero visits Maximus in his cell. Maximus learns his army is in Ostia and asks Cicero to do something for him.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Reunites with Cicero and Plans Rebellion
content_desc.summary_paragraph: While imprisoned behind iron bars, Maximus is secretly visited by his loyal friend Cicero. They share a heartfelt reunion, having both thought the other might be dead. Maximus quickly shifts to strategy, inquiring about the status of his loyal troops stationed in Ostia. Upon hearing they are ready to fight for him, Maximus prepares to ask Cicero for a crucial favor.

dialogue_highlights: I thought you were dead. Close.
How soon do you think they could be ready to fight? For you, tomorrow.
I need you to do something for me.

scenes.summary: Cicero intercepts Lucilla in the marketplace, passing a message from Maximus that he will meet her politician.

content_desc.clip_title: Cicero Delivers a Secret Message to Lucilla
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a bustling Roman market, street performers act out Maximus's victories in the arena to an amused crowd. Cicero spots Lucilla's heavily guarded litter passing through the streets and pushes his way forward. Despite the guards' attempts to hold him back, he manages to tell Lucilla that he still serves Maximus. She halts the procession to hear his message, learning that Maximus has agreed to meet her politician, and rewards Cicero for his loyalty.

dialogue_highlights: And I served the general, Maximus. I serve him still.
The general sends word, he will meet your politician.
For your loyalty, soldier.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene playing the role of Lucilla.

scenes.summary: Gracchus meets with Maximus. Maximus proposes a plan to escape, join his army in Ostia, and return to overthrow Commodus, promising to hand power back to the Senate.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Plans a Coup with Senator Gracchus
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a dimly lit cell, Maximus meets secretly with Senator Gracchus and Lucilla. He outlines a daring plan to escape Rome, reunite with his loyal army, and return to overthrow Commodus. Despite initial skepticism about trading one dictator for another, Gracchus agrees to fund the escape, moved by Maximus's loyalty to the late Emperor's vision.

dialogue_highlights: I will kill Commodus. The fate of Rome I leave to you.
Because that was the last wish of a dying man.
I will not trade one dictatorship for another!

scenes.summary: Gracchus pays Proximo to help Maximus escape. After Gracchus leaves, Proximo expresses his doubts to Maximus, reminding him that he is an entertainer, not a man of honor.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Convinces Proximo to Help Him Escape
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a tense exchange, Maximus attempts to secure Proximo's help in his plot against Emperor Commodus. Proximo is reluctant, pointing out the risks and his own profitable relationship with the Emperor. Maximus challenges Proximo's cynicism by appealing to his past and revealing that Commodus murdered Marcus Aurelius, the emperor who granted Proximo his freedom.

dialogue_highlights: I will kill Commodus.
He killed the man who set you free.
Do you remember what it was to have trust, Proximo?

trivia.text: Before Russell Crowe was cast as Maximus, several other actors were considered for the role, including Antonio Banderas, Tom Cruise and Mel Gibson, who turned down the role as he was already committed to The Patriot (2000).
trivia.match_reasoning: Russell Crowe is visible in this scene playing the role of Maximus.

trivia.text: Oliver Reed, who plays Proximo, died of a heart attack on May 2, before all his scenes had been filmed.
trivia.match_reasoning: Oliver Reed is visible in this scene playing the role of Proximo.

scenes.summary: Praetorian guards arrive at the gladiator school, signaling danger.

content_desc.clip_title: Praetorian Guards Arrive at Proximo's Compound
content_desc.summary_paragraph: An older man, Proximo, is warned by a servant about the arrival of Praetorian guards. The heavily armored soldiers march ominously into a sunlit courtyard filled with statues and poultry. The scene ends as the guards are ordered to halt, signaling an impending confrontation.

setting.location: Roman courtyard with stone arches and statues

dialogue_highlights: Praetorians, master.
Halt!

trivia.text: The scenes of slavery, desert travel, and the gladiatorial training school were shot in Ouarzazate, Morocco.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip takes place at the gladiatorial training school, which this fact notes was filmed in Ouarzazate, Morocco.

trivia.text: Oliver Reed, who plays Proximo, died of a heart attack on May 2, before all his scenes had been filmed.
trivia.match_reasoning: Oliver Reed is visible in the clip playing the character Proximo.

trivia.text: His character was meant to survive, but after Reed's death the script was revised to include Proximo's death at the hands of the Praetorian Guard.
trivia.match_reasoning: The Praetorian Guard, who ultimately kill Proximo due to script revisions after Reed's death, are seen arriving at the school in this clip.

scenes.summary: Commodus confronts Lucilla in his bedroom, revealing he knows of the plot and has arrested Gracchus. He forces her to submit to him, threatening her son Lucius.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Intimidates Lucilla in Her Bedroom
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Commodus confronts his sister Lucilla in her bedroom, questioning her about Senator Gracchus and expressing his intent to purge the Senate. The conversation takes a dark and uncomfortable turn as Commodus forces his incestuous affections upon a terrified Lucilla. He kisses her neck and face, demanding she acknowledge his love while she complies out of fear for her survival.

setting.location: Lavish bedroom in the imperial palace

dialogue_highlights: For the health of Rome, the Senate must be bled.
And I have only you to share it with.
You know I love you.

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix appears in this scene as Commodus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene as Lucilla.

scenes.summary: Lucilla visits Maximus in his cell to warn him that Gracchus has been arrested and they must execute the escape plan tonight. They share a tender moment before she leaves.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus and Lucilla Plan an Escape
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus is resting in his cell when guards open the door and Proximo enters, hinting at a plan in motion. Lucilla then arrives in secret to inform Maximus that her brother has arrested Gracchus and they must execute their escape plan tonight. They share a tender, emotional moment reflecting on their past love, culminating in a passionate kiss before she must leave.

key_actions: Maximus and Lucilla discussing their past
dialogue_highlights: My brother has had Gracchus arrested. We daren't wait any longer. We must leave tonight.
Because my father loved you. And because I loved you.
I have felt alone all my life, except with you.

trivia.text: Before Russell Crowe was cast as Maximus, several other actors were considered for the role, including Antonio Banderas, Tom Cruise and Mel Gibson, who turned down the role as he was already committed to The Patriot (2000).
trivia.match_reasoning: Russell Crowe is visible in the scene playing the role of Maximus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen is visible in the scene playing the role of Lucilla.

scenes.summary: Commodus tells Lucius a thinly veiled story about a traitorous sister. Lucilla listens in terror, realizing her plot to overthrow him has been discovered.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Threatens Lucilla Through Lucius
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Commodus finds young Lucius playing gladiator and learns the boy idolizes Maximus. Later, as Lucilla enters the room, Commodus tells Lucius a thinly veiled story about Emperor Claudius and a 'busy little bee'. The story serves as a terrifying threat to Lucilla, warning her that he knows of her betrayal and will harm her son if she does not confess.

dialogue_highlights: I'm Maximus, the savior of Rome!
Tell me what you've been doing, busy little bee. Or I shall strike down those dearest to you.

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix appears in this scene as Commodus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene as Lucilla.

scenes.summary: Praetorian guards storm Proximo's gladiator school. Proximo sacrifices himself to buy Maximus time to escape, while the other gladiators fight the guards.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Escapes as Proximo Sacrifices Himself
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Under the cover of night, Roman soldiers march to the gladiator barracks to arrest Maximus. Proximo hands Maximus the keys to escape, choosing to stay behind and face the consequences. Maximus's fellow gladiators bravely engage the soldiers in combat to buy him time to flee through an underground tunnel. As Maximus makes his escape, Proximo is surrounded and executed by the Roman guards.

dialogue_highlights: Proximo, are you in danger of becoming a good man?
Strength and honor.
Shadows and dust.

trivia.text: His character was meant to survive, but after Reed's death the script was revised to include Proximo's death at the hands of the Praetorian Guard.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip depicts the exact moment described in the revised script: Proximo's death at the hands of the Praetorian Guard.

trivia.text: To make it appear that Reed had performed the entirety of Proximo's scenes, a body double was used, and Reed's face was digitally attached to the body of the double in post-production.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features Proximo's final moments, which were famously completed using a body double and digital face replacement due to Oliver Reed's passing.

scenes.summary: Maximus escapes through the tunnels and finds his servant Cicero hanged. He is ambushed and captured by the Praetorian guards.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus Escapes the Tunnels and Falls into an Ambush
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus flees a chaotic battle through a dark, torch-lit tunnel. Emerging into a misty gorge, he hears a familiar birdcall and discovers his friend Cicero hanging from a tree. Cicero apologizes just before being struck by arrows, as Roman soldiers spring their trap on Maximus.

dialogue_highlights: Maximus!
I'm sorry.
No!

scenes.summary: At dawn, Commodus looks out over Rome as Falco informs him that the conspirators have been dealt with.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Contemplates Mercy at Sunset
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene opens with a sweeping view of ancient Rome at dusk, birds circling the Colosseum. Commodus stands on a balcony overlooking the city when an associate approaches to confirm a deed is done. Turning away from the view, Commodus coldly ponders the fate of his sister and nephew, debating whether to show them mercy or share their lover's fate.

dialogue_highlights: It's done.
Should they share her lover's fate? Or should I be merciful?
Commodus the Merciful.

trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix is shown in this scene playing the role of Commodus.

scenes.summary: Commodus confronts Lucilla, threatening Lucius's life unless she remains loyal and provides him with a pure-blooded heir.

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Threatens Lucilla for an Heir
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Commodus outlines his cruel ultimatum regarding the fate of young Lucius to ensure Lucilla's compliance. He then approaches a terrified and weeping Lucilla, demanding she love him and bear him a pure-blood heir to secure his dynasty. The scene culminates in Commodus aggressively grabbing her face and screaming, demanding to know if he is not merciful.

dialogue_highlights: You will provide me with an heir of pure blood, so that Commodus and his progeny will rule for 1,000 years.
Am I not merciful!

trivia.text: Jude Law auditioned for Commodus, but Joaquin Phoenix was offered the part after sending in a "knockout" audition tape.
trivia.match_reasoning: Joaquin Phoenix appears in this scene as Commodus.

trivia.text: Jennifer Lopez reportedly auditioned for Lucilla, but the role went to Connie Nielsen.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene as Lucilla.

scenes.summary: As the crowd chants for Maximus, Commodus visits him in his holding cell. Commodus stabs Maximus in the side to weaken him before their duel.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Commodus Stabs Maximus Before Their Arena Duel
content_desc.summary_paragraph: As the Colosseum crowd chants his name, Maximus is held in chains beneath the arena. Emperor Commodus confronts him, taunting him about his impending death and their shared connection to Marcus Aurelius. In a cowardly act of betrayal, Commodus embraces Maximus and stabs him in the side, ordering his guards to conceal the fatal wound before they enter the arena to fight.

key_actions: Guards preparing Maximus for the arena
dialogue_highlights: I think you have been afraid all your life.
I knew a man who once said, 'Death smiles at us all. All a man can do is smile back.'
Smile for me now, brother.

scenes.summary: Maximus and Commodus fight to the death in the Colosseum. Despite his poisoned wound, Maximus disarms and kills Commodus. With his dying breath, Maximus orders his men freed and Senator Gracchus reinstated, fulfilling Marcus Aurelius's dream for Rome. Lucilla honors Maximus, and his body is carried out of the arena by his allies.

content_desc.clip_title: Maximus defeats Commodus and dies honorably in the Colosseum.
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Maximus and Commodus engage in a brutal, final duel in the center of the Colosseum, surrounded by Praetorian Guards. When Commodus loses his weapon and demands another, Quintus orders the guards to sheathe their swords, leaving the emperor defenseless. Maximus overpowers and kills Commodus, but soon collapses from his own fatal wounds. As he dies, he experiences a peaceful vision of reuniting with his family in the afterlife. Lucilla rushes to his side, honors his sacrifice for Rome, and commands that he be carried out as a soldier.

dialogue_highlights: Sword! Give me a sword! Sheathe your swords. Sheathe your swords!
There was a dream that was Rome. It shall be realized.
Is Rome worth one good man's life? We believed it once. Make us believe it again. He was a soldier of Rome. Honor him.
sound_design: Ethereal, emotional music during the vision

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in the Colosseum, which was partially built as a physical set in Malta.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The digital extensions used to complete the upper tiers of the Colosseum are visible in the wide shots of the arena.

trivia.text: The scenes of Maximus' farm were filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.
trivia.match_reasoning: As Maximus dies, the scene cuts to visions of his farm (showing wheat fields and a dirt road), which were filmed in Val d'Orcia, Italy.

scenes.summary: In the empty Colosseum, Juba buries Maximus's family figurines in the sand. He looks up at the sky and promises his fallen friend that they will meet again, but not yet.

content_desc.clip_title: Juba Buries the Figurines in the Colosseum
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In the aftermath of the final gladiatorial battle, Juba kneels alone in the blood-stained sand of the empty Colosseum. He carefully buries the small wooden figurines representing Maximus's family, finding closure for his fallen friend. Looking out over the arena, he speaks to Maximus, expressing hope for a future reunion in the afterlife before walking away as the sun sets over Rome.

dialogue_highlights: Now we are free.
I will see you again. But not yet. Not yet.

trivia.text: The scenes set in Rome were shot in Malta, where the crew built a replica of about one-third of the Colosseum to a height of 52 feet (16 meters).
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip concludes with a wide shot of the Colosseum, which was partially built as a physical replica for the film's Rome scenes.

trivia.text: The other two-thirds and remaining height were added digitally.
trivia.match_reasoning: The wide shot of the Colosseum at the end of the clip showcases the digital extension used to complete the upper tiers and the rest of the arena.

scenes.summary: The end credits roll.
